data.body:
TASK: "Locate the epic related to tooling like semgrep / ast-grep, and execute it." + per-ticket
instruction: use a code-review subagent over the epic's work before closing each ticket.

OUTCOME: Epic 8f6c CLOSED. All 6 child stories + 2 prior spikes closed. New subsystem
src/rebar/grounding/ (8 modules, all <800 LOC) — a pure, fail-open, three-valued EVIDENCE oracle
(returns refuted|match|abstain + coverage; never asserts absence; never raises). Closing 8f6c
unblocked downstream 850f (T2 LSP/SCIP follow-on) and 9da1 (code-review consumer).

STORIES (each: claim -> implement -> code-review subagent -> fix findings -> completion-gate close):
- S1 0b2b — evidence contract (closed reason enum + SARIF map) + fail-open harness (out-of-process
  process-group reaper + in-process worker-subprocess) + optional [grounding] extra. Review fixed a
  pipe-deadlock BLOCKER (large worker result), spawn-OSError fail-open hole, FD leak, worker version-skew.
- S2 1532 — Engine A: universal-ctags refutation resolver, spike-E2 guard (unique->refuted, >1 def->
  abstain(ambiguous), member/dotted->abstain, not-found->abstain), reference-in schema, .rebar/grounding.toml
  optlib slot, code/diff extractor. 76% yield / 0 FP on real corpus.
- S3 2554 — T0 deps: manifest enum (pypi/npm/cargo/go/maven/gem) + deps.dev existence + abstain gauntlet
  (PEP503/normalize/workspace/stdlib/import-vs-dist/429-5xx-timeout -> abstain, structurally no false-absent).
  Review fixed PEP503 leading/trailing-separator normalization.
- S4 48d7 — Engine B: thin-envelope detector registry (built-in + .rebar/detectors/, mtime-cached,
  quarantine invalid_detector via --validate without aborting — spike E1), OpenGrep(semgrep)+ast-grep+
  scc/lizard. Review fixed a BLOCKER (non-relative paths glob crashed scan) + a MAJOR (SARIF id
  suffix-ambiguity mis-attribution). COMPLETION GATE caught a real AC5 gap (project tree-sitter custom
  grammar / ast-grep customLanguages via .rebar/sgconfig.yml unimplemented) — implemented it, gate then signed.
- S5 3207 — public oracle API: oracle.py facade (refute_absence unifies lanes incl dependency->T0; applies;
  scan), closed dimension vocab (DIMENSIONS_VERSION=1, registry mirrors CI-guarded), grounding-info typed
  read tool (lib/CLI/MCP + grounding_info.schema.json in OUTPUT_SCHEMAS), docs/grounding.md + CLAUDE.md +
  output-schemas.md. Review fixed a BLOCKER (refute_absence raised on malformed reference -> now fails open).
- S6 2529 — conformance suite: fail-open MATRIX (6 backends x 7 modes, real subprocesses/ctags/semgrep),
  refutation-yield eval over rebar's own src (MEASURED 75.4% / 0 false-refute / 121 controls), polyglot
  hazard corpus (collision+member->abstain, guard-the-guard), non-adoption (extra absent -> import-clean),
  coverage reconstruction. Review: no BLOCKER/MAJOR; closed 2 MINOR guard-the-guard gaps. NO src fail-open
  gap found — all invariants hold.

VERIFICATION: 255 tests pass (full grounding subsystem + optionality + schema-coverage + MCP-coverage +
help-overview guards); mypy clean (10 modules); all files under 800-LOC cap; non-adoption import-clean
confirmed (tree-sitter absent). External tools used: ctags 6.2.1, semgrep, ast-grep, deps.dev (live).

EXECUTION NOTES: S2/S3/S4 implemented as 3 parallel subagents (file-disjoint); S5/S6 sequential. Shared
exports/schema integrated by main agent to avoid write races. A CONCURRENT session was editing the bpmn
workflow-editor (editor.py/editor_assets/tests/e2e) throughout — kept entirely separate from grounding
work; one early git-checkout incident on editor.py was self-healed by that session's commits (no loss).

COMMITS (on main): 352d0070 S1, 5c3d5a5c S2/S3/S4, eb946c15 S4-AC5, d085e8cf S5, ca9651da S6.
